note_strategy.name: slightly_awkward_compliment
note_strategy.description: Reference something specific about the person's LinkedIn profile — a recent post topic, an unusual headline observation, or a genuine niche interest. Must feel written by a human who actually looked at their profile. Avoids the AI-slop pattern of generic praise ('love your work', 'impressed by your journey'). One authentic observation + low-key reason to connect. No pitch in the note — relationship first.
note_type_priority: post_reference — reference a specific post from last 60 days (highest engagement rate — HANDS checks posts tab during search execution)
headline — reference something specific or unusual in their profile headline
interest — reference a genuine niche or hobby listed on their profile
generic — fallback if no posts, headline is bland, and no interests visible
hard_constraints: Under 200 characters (LinkedIn free account limit for connection note)
No mention of email campaign or any prior outreach
No pitch, no service offer in the connection note
No question marks — declarative or statement-based ending ('Good to connect.' not 'Would love to connect?')
No 'I came across your profile' — too generic
IMAP check per lead before sending connection request
Do NOT send if lead replied NOT_INTERESTED or UNSUBSCRIBE in email channel
note_strategy.character_count_note: 200 chars is approx 30-35 words. Keep it to 3 clauses max. Count includes spaces and punctuation.

active_notes_note: Zero active notes. LinkedIn permanently closed for all Batch 5 leads. linkedin_profiles.json never written in 66 campaign sessions. No active leads with profile data. Batch 6 note templates below — activated Day 3 (Fri 2026-06-12 Scenario B, or Fri 2026-06-19 Scenario C) once HANDS writes batch_6_leads.json (LAST VIABLE WINDOW TODAY Monday EOD for Scenario B) and finds LinkedIn profiles via orchestrator.py.

sector_templates.note: 5 sector-level templates. HANDS selects the most appropriate for each lead, then personalises using actual profile data (post topic, headline text, interest). Confirm under 200 chars before sending. Templates are the starting point — not the final note.

trades_post_reference.template: Saw your post about [specific topic from post]. Refreshing to see a [trade] in [town] talking about [specific element] — most don't. [Name], good to connect.
trades_post_reference.example: Saw your post about underfloor heating jobs in older stone properties. Refreshing to see a Skipton plumber talking about the quirks of Dales builds. David, good to connect.
personalisation_notes: Use the actual post topic — be specific. 'saw your post about boiler installs' is too generic.
Reference the local angle if the post mentions a local job or challenge.
Name at the end is warmer than the start — feels less like a template.

trades_headline.template: Not many [trade]s describe themselves as '[specific unusual element from headline]'. Caught my eye. [Name], good to connect.
trades_headline.example: Not many roofers describe themselves as 'heritage slate specialist'. Caught my eye. James, good to connect.
personalisation_notes: Only use if the headline contains something genuinely specific or unusual.
If the headline is generic ('Plumber | Gas Safe | Harrogate'), use trades_generic instead.
Quote the actual headline text — shows you read it.

trades_generic.template: Based in [town], working with local trades businesses. Thought it worth connecting — [Name], always good to know the decent [trade]s in [area].
trades_generic.example: Based in Knaresborough, working with local trades businesses. Thought it worth connecting — Mark, always good to know the decent plumbers in the area.
personalisation_notes: Fallback only — use post_reference or headline templates if any profile data available.
Keep the [area] vague enough to feel natural ('the area', 'North Yorkshire', not 'Knaresborough and surrounding villages').

professional_services_post_reference.template: Your post about [specific topic] was the most honest take on [topic area] I've seen from a [profession] in a while. [Name], good to connect.
professional_services_post_reference.example: Your post about conveyancing delays in the current market was the most honest take on the issue I've seen from a solicitor in a while. Sarah, good to connect.
personalisation_notes: Professional services contacts value being seen as thoughtful, not just skilled.
The 'most honest take' framing is the slightly awkward compliment — implies others aren't honest.
Only use if the post is genuinely substantive (opinion/insight, not just sharing news).

hospitality_post_reference.template: Saw your post about [specific topic]. Running a [business type] in [town] and still finding time to write that — respect. [Name], good to connect.
hospitality_post_reference.example: Saw your post about the August bank holiday rush and how you handle it solo. Running a B&B in Grassington and still finding time to write that — respect. Julie, good to connect.
personalisation_notes: Hospitality owners post rarely — if they do, acknowledge the effort explicitly.
The 'respect' line is slightly awkward in a good way — unpretentious.
Stay under 200 chars — this template runs long with specific post topics.

batch6_placeholder_notes.note: 19 placeholder entries for Batch 6 leads (BATCH6_P001-P019). HANDS populates each with actual LinkedIn profile data on Day 3 execution (Fri 2026-06-12 Scenario B, or Fri 2026-06-19 Scenario C). Process per lead: (1) Run google_query from linkedin_search_plan.json. (2) Identify profile — confirm owner not employee. (3) Check posts tab (last 60 days). (4) Select appropriate sector_template above. (5) Personalise using profile data. (6) Confirm under 200 chars. (7) Send note via orchestrator.py. (8) Write linkedin_profiles.json entry IMMEDIATELY — do NOT batch.

minimum_viable_features: Drive Playwright: Google search → extract LinkedIn profile URL
Confirm profile is business owner (not employee)
Check posts tab (last 60 days) → flag if post found
Read note template from data/linkedin_connection_notes.json → personalise using profile data
Write data/linkedin_profiles.json entry IMMEDIATELY after each attempt — do NOT batch
Log errors + skip reasons — never crash silently
